title: Decision: viewer search accepts natural-language graph queries
summary: Decision: the viewer search bar should behave like lightweight graph search, not exact substring search. Natural language questions such as 'Where is the memory about running tests?' are tokenized, stop words are removed
body:
Decision: the viewer search bar should behave like lightweight graph search, not exact substring search. Natural-language questions such as 'Where is the memory about running tests?' are tokenized, stop words are removed, simple stems and synonyms are applied, and terms are matched as concept groups so run/running/command/npm and test/tests/vitest/jest can find runbook or command memories.
Evidence: Updated mcp/viewer/app.js search parsing and matching after the exact user query returned no results.
Verified by: node --check mcp/viewer/app.js; npm test --prefix mcp; npm --cache /tmp/kage-npm-cache pack --dry-run
